San Andreas (2015)

Theatrical Performance
Domestic Box Office $155,190,832Details
International Box Office $319,400,000
Worldwide Box Office $474,590,832
Domestic Physical Disc Sales
DVD Sales (estimated) $21,256,001
Blu-ray Sales (estimated) $23,113,318
Total $44,369,319
Further financial details...

Synopsis

After the infamous San Andreas Fault gives, triggering a magnitude 9-plus earthquake in California, a search and rescue pilot and his estranged wife make their way together from Los Angeles to San Francisco to save their only daughter. But their treacherous journey north is only the beginning. And when they think the worst may be over...it's just getting started.

Metrics

Opening Weekend:$54,588,173 (35.2% of total gross)
Legs:2.84 (domestic box office/biggest weekend)
Domestic Share:32.7% (domestic box office/worldwide)
Production Budget:$110,000,000 (worldwide box office is 4.3 times production budget)
Theater counts:3,777 opening theaters/3,812 max. theaters, 5.8 weeks average run per theater
Infl. Adj. Dom. BO $220,175,838

Movie Details

Domestic Releases: May 29th, 2015 (Wide) by Warner Bros.
International Releases: May 27th, 2015 (Wide) (France)
May 27th, 2015 (Wide) (Belgium)
May 28th, 2015 (Wide) (Uruguay)
May 28th, 2015 (Wide) (Netherlands)
May 28th, 2015 (Wide) (Peru)
Video Release: September 22nd, 2015 by Warner Home Video
MPA Rating: PG-13 for intense disaster action and mayhem throughout, and brief strong language.
(Rating bulletin 2367, 4/1/2015)
Running Time: 114 minutes
Film format: 3-D, 3-D - Post-production Conversion, IMAX: DMR

Plot point: Disaster, Rescue
Subgenre: Survival Adventure

Genre:Adventure
Production Method:Live Action
Creative Type:Science Fiction

Production Countries: United States
Languages: English

Synopsis

After the infamous San Andreas Fault gives, triggering a magnitude 9-plus earthquake in California, a search and rescue pilot and his estranged wife make their way together from Los Angeles to San Francisco to save their only daughter. But their treacherous journey north is only the beginning. And when they think the worst may be over...it's just getting started.
